title: Israel-Iran missile exchanges raise fears of fresh regional escalation
subhead: Iran launched ballistic missiles toward Israel in response to an Israeli airstrike on Beirut's southern suburbs, prompting an immediate Israeli retaliation on Iranian territory.

subhead: The hard-left four-time presidential candidate held his first campaign rally in Saint-Denis on Sunday.

title: Israeli filmmaker Nadav Lapid withdraws from Marseille festival after boycott controversy
subhead: Several filmmakers had decided to pull their films from the event, scheduled for July, to protest the director's presence.

title: Working-class landmarks in the Paris region are being taken over by luxury and art powers
subhead: Projects such as Le Large, built on a historic Renault factory site on Seguin island, or at the Maison du Peuple in Paris's northwestern suburbs, illustrate how major corporations are taking over former industrial sites.

title: Farida Rustamova: 'Putin is at an impasse, unable to win or abandon this war'
subhead: Despite the Russian president's refusal to meet with his Ukrainian counterpart, exiled Russian political scientist Farida Rustamova sees signs that the Russian leader may be open to negotiations, which she shares in an interview.

title: Bernadette Chirac, the wife of ex-president Jacques Chirac, has died
subhead: An unfailing supporter of her husband's career, Bernadette Chodron de Courcel became a key figure a French politics. She was elected local councilor, a rare feat for a 'wife of,' and was very involved in charity work. She died at the age of 93.

title: 'We must hold our heads high!': The fears and faint hopes of Israel's peace activists
subhead: Every day, a few thousand NGO members act in support of peaceful coexistence with Palestinians, a halt to settlements and an end to the military occupation of the West Bank. Yet, their political and social influence has been steadily declining over the past 20 years.
